introduction:
The Ration Card is an official document issued by the Maharashtra Food authority of india, Civil Supplies and Consumer Protection Department. It entitles eligible families to subsidised food grains (rice, wheat, sugar) under the National Food Security Act (NFSA). It is also widely accepted as a valid address and identity proof across India.

The ration card records the head of family, all family members, category (APL / BPL / AAY), and the allotted Fair Price Shop (FPS). Every family is entitled to exactly one ration card.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Applying for a new ration card is FREE of cost
  • Processing time is approximately 30 working days
  • You must be a permanent resident of Maharashtra to apply
  • Only one ration card per family is allowed
  • Track your application at rcms.mahafood.gov.in using your reference number
  • Aadhaar linking is mandatory for all family members
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Ration Card

Note: You need to provide documents for Identity Proof, Address Proof, and Income Certificate. Some documents serve multiple purposes.
Identity Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
  • Government Employee ID
Address Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Electricity Bill (recent)
  • Telephone Bill (recent)
  • Bank Passbook
  • Registered Rent Agreement
  • Property Tax Receipt
Income & Family Proof
  • Income Certificate from Tehsildar/SDO
  • Birth Certificates of all children
  • Marriage Certificate (if applicable)
  • Passport-size photos of all family members
Category Guide
  • AAY (Yellow Card): Poorest of poor families
  • BPL (Orange Card): Income below ₹1 lakh/year
  • APL (White Card): Above poverty line
  • Annapurna (Green): Senior citizens (65+) without pension

Note: BPL/AAY require income certificate.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Aaple Sarkar Portal

Maharashtra provides an online facility through Aaple Sarkar and the Ration Card Management System (RCMS). You need an Aadhaar-linked mobile number.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in or rcms.mahafood.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Click "New User? Register Here". Enter your Aadhaar number and mobile number. Verify via OTP and set a password.
  3. Select the Service
    After login go to Food & Civil Supplies → "New Ration Card Application"
  4. Fill Application Form
    Enter all family member details accurately:
    • Head of family name (as per Aadhaar)
    • All family members with their Aadhaar numbers
    • Select ration card category (APL / BPL / AAY)
    • Select your Fair Price Shop (FPS) from the dropdown
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of identity proof, address proof, income certificate, and passport photos. File size limit: 200 KB per document (JPG/PDF only)
  6. Submit & Save Reference Number
    Click Submit. An Application Reference Number will be sent via SMS. Save this number!
  7. Verification Visit
    A field inspector will visit your home within 15 working days for physical verification.
Tip: Keep all original documents ready at home during the inspector's verification visit.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Tehsil / Taluka Office (Direct Visit)

You can directly visit your local Taluka / Tehsil office to apply without internet access.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Taluka / Tehsil Office
    Go to your local office during working hours: Monday – Saturday, 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (2nd and 4th Saturdays are holidays)
  2. Collect Application Form (RC-1)
    Ask for Form RC-1 (New Ration Card Application) from the counter. The form is provided free of cost.
  3. Fill the Form Carefully
    Use black ink and BLOCK LETTERS. Enter:
    • Head of family full name (as per Aadhaar)
    • All family member details & Aadhaar numbers
    • Residential address and ward/village
    • Category required (APL/BPL/AAY)
    • Active mobile number
  4. Attach Self-Attested Documents
    Attach photocopies of all required documents. Bring original documents for counter verification.
  5. Submit at the Counter
    Submit the form + documents. The officer gives you an Acknowledgement Receipt with your application number.
  6. Home Verification
    A field officer visits your address within 15 working days.
  7. Collect Your Ration Card
    Collect from the Taluka office or receive by post within 30 working days of approval.
Processing Time: Approximately 30 working days from the date of application submission.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Your Application

Visit rcms.mahafood.gov.in
Enter your Application Reference Number to check current status.

Download Digital Ration Card

Once approved, download your ration card from the Aaple Sarkar or RCMS portal. The digital copy is valid for all government purposes.

Link Aadhaar to Ration Card

Mandatory for all family members. Visit your Fair Price Shop or the RCMS portal to complete Aadhaar seeding for uninterrupted grain benefits.

Raise a Grievance

If not processed within 30 days, call Toll-Free: 1967 or visit mahafood.gov.in to file an online complaint.
